* Decedents for whom the International Classification of Diseases (ICD) code for asbestosis was listed as the underlying cause of death were identified from 1968–2005 mortality data. ICDA-8 code 515.2 (asbestosis) for years 1968–1978, ICD-9 code 501 (asbestosis) for years 1979–1998, and ICD-10 code J61 (pneumoconiosis due to asbestos and other mineral fibers) for years 1999–2005. For years 1999–2005, decedents with ICD-10 underlying cause coded as J65 (pneumoconiosis associated with tuberculosis) or J92.0 (pleural plaque with presence of asbestos) also were included in the underlying cause of death tabulation for asbestosis if code J61 also was listed on the death certificate.

* Decedents for whom the International Classification of Diseases (ICD) code for asbestosis was listed as the underlying cause of death were identified from mortality data using ICD-9 code 501 (asbestosis) for years 1979–1998, and ICD-10 code J61 (pneumoconiosis due to asbestos and other mineral fibers) for 1999. For 1999, decedents with ICD-10 underlying cause coded as J65 (pneumoconiosis associated with tuberculosis) or J92.0 (pleural plaque with presence of asbestos) also were included in the underlying cause of death tabulation for asbestosis if code J61 also was listed on the death certificate.
† Alaska, Colorado, Georgia, Hawaii, Idaho, Indiana, Kansas, Kentucky, Maine, Missouri, Nebraska, Nevada, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New Mexico, North Carolina, Ohio, Oklahoma, Rhode Island, South Carolina, Tennessee, Utah, Vermont, Washington, West Virginia, and Wisconsin.
§ Industry and occupation information from death certificates was coded on the NCHS multiple cause-of-death data files according to the 1980 U.S. Bureau of Census Index of Industries and Occupations classification system from 1985 to 1992, and according to the 1990 U.S. Bureau of Census classification system from 1993 to 1999. For the industries and occupations listed, the 1980 and 1990 classification system codes and titles were the same.

checks.label: HTTPS (required for service workers)
checks.why: HTTPS is required for service workers and most install prompts.

checks.label: Web app manifest declared
checks.why: A web app manifest is required for installability and controls how the app appears when launched.

checks.label: Service worker registered
checks.why: A service worker is what makes offline access, caching, and background sync possible.

checks.label: Theme color declared
checks.why: Theme color controls the browser chrome/status-bar color when the site is installed or bookmarked.

checks.label: Apple touch icon (iOS/bookmark icon)
checks.why: iOS uses this icon for home-screen bookmarks and "Add to Home Screen" — without it the icon falls back to a screenshot.

checks.label: Manifest fetched and parsed successfully
checks.why: A manifest that fails to parse cannot drive installability or launch behavior even if the link is present.

checks.label: Manifest declares a maskable icon
checks.why: A maskable icon lets the OS safely crop/mask the app icon on different device shapes without clipping content.

checks.label: Service worker is active (not just registered)
checks.why: An active service worker (not just registered) is what actually intercepts requests and serves cached content.

checks.label: Service worker controls this page load
checks.why: A controlling service worker means THIS page load is being served/intercepted, not just a future one.

checks.label: Meets basic installability criteria
checks.evidence: no readable web app manifest; no service worker registered
checks.why: Determines whether the browser can realistically offer "Add to Home Screen" / install, based on manifest + service worker + HTTPS together.
